senses.definition: to die or no longer exist

examples.x: The old gunslinger faced off with the outlaw, and the outlaw was the one who bit the dust.
After a long and courageous battle with cancer, she finally bit the dust, leaving her family in mourning.
The ambitious business venture ultimately bit the dust due to a lack of funding and support.

senses.definition: to die, used humorously

examples.x: When the old girl finally kicked the bucket there was no mention of yours truly in the will.
He always said he wanted to travel the world before kicking the bucket, and he did just that.
After a long and fulfilling life, the elderly woman finally kicked the bucket in her sleep.
The old car had seen better days and finally kicked the bucket on a remote country road.
The aging actor had a heart attack and tragically kicked the bucket in the middle of a live performance.

senses.definition: (of a person, animal, or plant) certainly not alive or in existence any longer

examples.x: The technology from that era is as dead as a dodo now, with no practical use in today's world.
The company's outdated business model became as dead as a dodo in the face of changing consumer preferences.
The once-popular fashion trend from the 1980s is now dead as a dodo, never to return.
After the advent of digital photography, film cameras became dead as a dodo in the consumer market.

word_list.pronunciation: æz ɔːɹ dˈɛd æz ɐ dˈoːɹneɪl ɔːɹ hˈɛɹɪŋ

senses.definition: dead without a doubt

examples.x: The cop announced that the body in the dumpster was dead as a doornail.
When the sons ran forward he was dead as a herring, with a broken neck.
After years of neglect, the old house was now dead as a doornail, with broken windows and a crumbling roof.
The proposal to build a new shopping mall was rejected, making the project as dead as a doornail.
When the engine of the old car sputtered to a stop, it was as dead as a doornail, leaving the driver stranded.

senses.definition: undoubtedly no longer alive or in existence

examples.x: When the batteries in the remote control died, it was as dead as mutton, and we couldn't change the channel.
After the old refrigerator stopped working, it became as dead as mutton, and all the food inside spoiled.
The abandoned factory on the outskirts of town is as dead as mutton, with no signs of life or activity.
The outdated software had so many bugs and glitches that it was dead as mutton in terms of usability.
The proposal for the new project was rejected by the investors, leaving the idea dead as mutton.

senses.definition: to cease to be alive

examples.x: Did you hear that old Walt bought the farm?
Unfortunately, when the mountaineer lost his footing, he fell to his death, buying the farm in a tragic accident.
The aging actor had a heart attack while on stage, and he bought the farm right in front of the audience.
When the old car finally gave out on the highway, the driver had to be towed, and it felt like the car had bought the farm.
In the crash landing, the pilot miraculously survived, but the plane bought the farm, becoming a complete wreck.

senses.definition: to die before a person retires from their job

examples.x: My grandfather was very hard working and brave and he died in harness.
Despite his retirement age, John continued to work and eventually died in harness at his beloved job.
The dedicated teacher didn't want to retire, so she continued to teach and died in harness in the classroom.
Even on his deathbed, the doctor was still advising his patients, embodying the spirit of dying in harness.

senses.definition: to stop living

examples.x: After a long battle with illness, the elderly man peacefully gave up the ghost in his sleep.
The old car finally sputtered and gave up the ghost, leaving its owner stranded on the side of the road.
When the power went out during the storm, all the electronic devices in the house gave up the ghost.
The ancient computer struggled to keep up with modern software and eventually gave up the ghost, refusing to start.
The marathon runner pushed himself to the limit and collapsed as he crossed the finish line, seemingly ready to give up the ghost.

senses.definition: the state of being close to death due to old age or a fatal sickness

examples.x: At 90 years old, my grandfather often jokes about having one foot in the grave, but he's still quite active.
After the severe heart attack, she felt like she had one foot in the grave but managed to make a remarkable recovery.
The patient's condition was so critical that the doctors believed he had one foot in the grave, but they worked tirelessly to save him.
The retired professor may have one foot in the grave, but his intellectual curiosity remains as vibrant as ever.
With his reckless lifestyle, some thought the rock star had one foot in the grave, but he surprised everyone by making a comeback.

senses.definition: to put the body of a person who has died in a tomb or grave during a funeral
to die, used in a humorous way
to complete one's unfinished business, such as clearing debts, etc., especially because one is about to die

examples.x: After receiving a terminal diagnosis, she began to put her affairs in order by updating her will and arranging her financial matters.
The aging businessman decided to retire and put his affairs in order by selling the company and distributing assets to his family.
Following his sudden passing, his family had to put his affairs in order by locating important documents and settling outstanding debts.
The elderly woman wanted to make things easier for her children, so she began to put her affairs in order well in advance of her expected retirement.

senses.definition: to lose one's life

examples.x: In her final moments, the hospice nurse reassured the terminally ill patient that he would soon get his ticket punched, finding peace in the afterlife.

senses.definition: to be in a very dangerous situation that one may face death at any moment

examples.x: We all thought we were done for when the boat started to sink.
After failing to submit the project on time, he felt that his chances for a good grade were done for.
The soccer team was trailing by five goals with only a few minutes left; they believed the game was done for.
When the unexpected storm hit the camping trip, they realized their outdoor adventure was done for and headed back home.

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is about to die

examples.x: After the car accident, he was rushed to the hospital, and for a time, it seemed like he was at death's door.
The elderly patient had been battling a serious illness for months and was now at death's door.
When the hiker was found after days of being lost in the wilderness, he was dehydrated, starving, and at death's door.
The doctor's quick intervention saved the child who had a severe allergic reaction and was at death's door.
After the shipwreck, the survivors spent days adrift at sea, becoming weak and at death's door before they were rescued.

senses.definition: to be expected to die soon because of a serious illness, terrible accident, etc.

examples.x: She lived on borrowed time for years before finally receiving a heart transplant.
The endangered species was living on borrowed time as their habitat continued to be destroyed.
After surviving the plane crash, he felt like he was living on borrowed time and decided to make the most of every moment.
The cancer patient, who had defied the odds for years, knew she was living on borrowed time but remained optimistic.
The reckless driver narrowly avoided a fatal accident, making him realize he had been living on borrowed time.

senses.definition: (of a large number of people or animals) to die or get sick rapidly

examples.x: In the war zone, soldiers were dying like flies in the fierce combat.
During the deadly pandemic, people in overcrowded areas were dropping like flies due to the fast-spreading virus.
The factory's unsafe conditions led to numerous accidents, with workers falling like flies.

senses.definition: to die, often after a long life or struggle

examples.x: After a long and adventurous life, the explorer cashed in his chips, leaving behind a legacy of exploration.
The elderly woman, who had been battling a terminal illness, decided it was time to cash in her chips with dignity.
When the aging actor passed away, it was said that he had finally cashed in his chips after a lifetime in the spotlight.
In the heat of battle, the soldier displayed immense courage and bravery, knowing that he might cash in his chips on the battlefield.
The terminally ill patient had made peace with the inevitable and was ready to cash in his chips, surrounded by loved ones.

senses.definition: to stop being alive

examples.x: Nobody lives in that house since old Roger popped his clogs.
Poor old Tom finally popped his clogs at the ripe old age of 95.
If I ever pop my clogs, I hope it's in my sleep, painlessly.
I heard that the famous actor popped his clogs during a live performance.
Don't worry about me, I don't plan to pop my clogs anytime soon!

senses.definition: to no longer be alive

examples.x: When I'm gone, don't mourn for me; I'll be pushing up daisies and at peace.
It's important to enjoy life while we're here because eventually, we'll all be pushing up the daisies.
She used to say that when she's pushing up daisies, she wanted a beautiful garden over her grave.
If I were you, I'd make the most of every day because none of us know when we'll be pushing up the daisies.
